Patch Set 4: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(8 inline comments)

Prakash, thanks for working up this patch.

The enhancements to lustre_rmmod and the lnet script themselves look fine, but I'd like to also keep the current behaviour even with the addition of the new options.  More detailed comments inline.

Patch Set 4: (3 inline comments)

Andreas, thanks for the quick review.

It looks like I can drop support for the naked "$1" parameter, but need to define no parameters to mean "unload all Lustre and backend-fs modules (ldiskfs, ZFS, etc.)".

My only remaining question is the error reporting, "lcl dl", and "lctl dk", steps were all modified by this patch. Do those need to be maintained?

Specifically, having the side effect of "lctl dk" just seems wrong. And now that you can do "lustre_rmmod -e libcfs && lctl dk && lustre_rmmod -i libcfs", which you couldn't do before, it's completely unnecessary.

"lct dl" and the error reporting just seemed like good cleanup at the time.

Patch Set 5:

I decided to drop the "-i" and "-e" functionality altogether. I realized I was never actually using "-e", so there really wasn't a compelling reason to keep it around, IMO.

I _think_ this version maintains enough backwards compatibility to work with previous test scripts.

Patch Set 12:

Is this patch just unlucky, or is there some underlying problem causing so many tests to fail?

Patch Set 12:

I'd like to think it's just "unlucky". I looked some of the latest failures, and I think they're resulting from this:

    21:19:09:Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mount -t lustre -o user_xattr,acl,flock wtm-13vm7@tcp:/lustre /mnt/lustre
    21:19:09:LustreError: 165-2: Nothing registered for client mount! Is the 'lustre' module loaded?
    21:19:09:LustreError: 17216:0:(obd_mount.c:1274:lustre_fill_super()) Unable to mount  (-19)

Although I don't immediately see how this patch would cause this.

parents.subject: LU-4054 llog: don't leak llog handle in llog_cat_process_cb()

commit.subject: LU-2669 scripts: Change behavior of lustre_rmmod
commit.message:
LU-2669 scripts: Change behavior of lustre_rmmod

The lustre_rmmod script was modified to take an arbitrary list of
modules and try to remove them and any modules dependent on them.
Previously its behavior was to always remove the libcfs module, along
with either the ldiskfs or another module passed as a parameter.

The old interface was roughly maintained. Any of the following commands
will remove the ldiskfs, libcfs, and all dependent modules:

    $ lustre_rmmod
    $ lustre_rmmod ldiskfs
    $ lustre_rmmod ldiskfs libcfs

The benefit now, is that any other list of modules can be specified
without removing libcfs. For example, the following command will only
remove ptlrpc and its dependent modules (leaving libcfs intact):

    $ lustre_rmmod ptlrpc

This was not previously possible. As a result, the lnet init script was
modified to use this new functionality instead of rolling its own
implementation to obtain the same result.

Signed-off-by: Prakash Surya <surya1@llnl.gov>
Change-Id: Id9732f399d1bc7d7d4f1ec4f684fa16cab35e786
